observation.summary: The C5 runtime scheduling entrypoint, invariant index, and core invariants still described scheduler boundaries as manually edited prose while task requirement, eligibility, local daemon, and SaaS source evidence already existed.

hypothesis.summary: A runtime-scheduling manifest can generate the entrypoint, invariant index, and core invariant page while linking the existing dedicated evidence surfaces.

execution.summary: Generate the C5 reader docs from SSOT and check source anchors for capability-only dispatch, pure runtime selection, pre-submit eligibility, lease pinning, source ownership, and read-model boundaries.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects generated doc drift, missing source anchors, missing workflow wiring, and missing references to lower-level runtime scheduling evidence.

retrospective.summary: The C5 overview surface now points readers to generated docs and dedicated evidence instead of asking agents to reinterpret prose-only scheduler invariants.

evidence.proves: runtime scheduling entrypoint, invariant index, and core invariant docs are generated from SSOT
public CI uploads runtime scheduling docs evidence
